purpose: Track ordered implementation work from the first-principles and reusable object-oriented design audit.

agentInstructions.selectionRule: Implement the first task whose status is not complete and whose dependencies are complete.
agentInstructions.branchRule: Create a focused branch from main for each task or small compatible batch.
agentInstructions.scopeRule: Keep each task behavior-preserving unless the acceptanceCriteria explicitly requires a behavior change.
agentInstructions.verificationRule: Run the listed verification commands plus any focused tests added or changed by the task.

tasks.title: Extract shared recorder selector policy
tasks.auditFinding: Selector Extraction Is Duplicated Across Recorder Runtime And Shared Core
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: Selector priority is a central recorder policy and must remain consistent between capture, conversion, tests, and eventual selector healing.
implementationNotes: Move DOM-safe helper functions for test id detection, accessible label detection, role inference, visible text extraction, CSS fallback, and escaping into a shared plain JavaScript module.
Import the shared module from both content-script.js and recorder-core.js without introducing build tooling.
Preserve current selector priority and any existing uncommitted recorder behavior.
Add or update smoke coverage proving both runtime capture and recording conversion use the same selector policy.
acceptanceCriteria: No duplicated targetFromElement, attr, visibleText, explicitOrInferredRole, cssSelector, or cssEscape implementations remain across content-script.js and recorder-core.js.
Recorder JSON import still converts actions into test-definition steps.
Existing support for data-testid and any already-added alternate test id attributes remains intact.
Smoke tests cover selector priority.
notes: MV3 content_scripts cannot declare type: module, so selector-policy.js is a plain UMD-style script assigned onto globalThis.RecorderSelectorPolicy rather than using ES import/export; content-script.js and recorder-core.js both read the same object.

Review subagent could not formally GitHub-approve its own PR (gh blocks self-approval for the same authenticated account) and posted its verdict as a comment instead; review was substantive (ran tests, diffed against main, empirically verified the new regression assertion) but carries no formal approval status.

tasks.title: Extract RunArtifactWriter from TestRunsService
tasks.auditFinding: TestRunsService Is Becoming An Orchestration God Object
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: Run lifecycle orchestration and artifact persistence change for different reasons.
implementationNotes: Move writeRunArtifacts and saveArtifact responsibilities out of TestRunsService.
Inject ArtifactStorageService and Artifact repository into the new writer service.
Keep artifact storage keys, placeholder behavior, content types, and best-effort failure handling behavior unchanged.
Leave broader run lifecycle extraction for a later task.
acceptanceCriteria: TestRunsService no longer directly writes artifact content or creates Artifact rows.
Run report, screenshot placeholder, trace placeholder, real screenshot, and real trace behavior remain unchanged.
Artifact persistence failure still appends a log entry without failing an otherwise completed run.
Focused tests pass.
verificationCommands: cd backend && npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/test-runs/test-runs.service.spec.ts src/test-runs/run-report.spec.ts
notes: Merged earlier on 2026-07-02 as PR #20 but the tracker was not updated at merge time; status backfilled in PR #23.

tasks.title: Extract dashboard workflow hooks
tasks.auditFinding: Frontend Page Components Own Too Much Workflow State
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: Dashboard data workflow and dashboard rendering change for different reasons.
implementationNotes: Extract project detail loading into useProjectDetail(projectId).
Extract pending-run polling into useRunPolling(projectId, runs, onRunsLoaded).
Keep JSX structure and visible UI behavior unchanged.
Prefer a small hook extraction before larger component splitting.
acceptanceCriteria: page.tsx no longer owns all project detail loading and run polling mechanics directly.
Pending runs still refresh about every 1500ms.
Project selection, test definition listing, run history, and refresh button behavior remain unchanged.
App shell smoke test still passes.
notes: Merged earlier on 2026-07-02 as PR #21 but the tracker was not updated at merge time; status backfilled in PR #23.

tasks.title: Convert step validation and dispatch toward a handler registry
tasks.auditFinding: Step Execution Is A Switch Statement Waiting For Polymorphism
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: Step type is the axis of variation for validation, dispatch behavior, logging, AI schema, recorder conversion, and frontend typing.
implementationNotes: Start with a behavior-preserving table or registry rather than a large class hierarchy.
Centralize per-step required fields and dispatch/log behavior where practical.
Avoid changing the external TestStep JSON shape.
Keep AI schema and frontend type synchronization visible if they remain separate.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing step types validate exactly as before.
Existing Playwright dispatch behavior and log text remain compatible with current tests.
Adding a future step type has a clearer single registration path than editing multiple switches.
Step dispatcher focused tests pass.
notes: Merged earlier on 2026-07-02 as PR #22 but the tracker was not updated at merge time; status backfilled in PR #23.
Implemented as backend/src/test-steps/step-registry.ts: one entry per step type holding required fields and Playwright execution behavior.

tasks.title: Introduce AI provider adapter boundary when provider variation grows
tasks.auditFinding: AI Provider Selection Wants Strategy, Not Branching Inside The Generator
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: Generating structured tests is stable, while provider transport, credentials, response shape, and error mapping vary independently.
implementationNotes: This task is optional until a third provider is added or current tests become painful.
Keep shared prompt construction, step validation, and base URL scoping centralized.
Move provider-specific API calls and response extraction behind adapters.
Do not change the public AI settings API.
acceptanceCriteria: AiTestGenerationService delegates provider-specific generation to adapters.
Anthropic and Ollama behavior remains unchanged.
Unsupported providers still fail clearly.
AI generation and settings tests pass.
verificationCommands: cd backend && npm test -- --runTestsByPath src/ai/ai-test-generation.service.spec.ts src/ai/ai-settings.service.spec.ts
notes: Defer if no third provider or provider-test pain exists yet.

Task notes marked this deferrable until a third provider existed; implemented now (at user direction to complete the list) with a deliberately minimal surface: AiProviderAdapter is one generateText method plus an isConfigured predicate.

Provider transport lives in anthropic-provider.adapter.ts and ollama-provider.adapter.ts, injected via the AI_PROVIDER_ADAPTERS token; the shared system prompt and output schema moved to generation-prompt.ts; prompt construction, validation, and base-URL scoping stay in AiTestGenerationService.
Behavior-preserving: all error messages, the 30s Ollama timeout, and the unsupported-provider 400 are unchanged.

tasks.title: Create or prepare a shared test-step kernel
tasks.auditFinding: Domain Types Are Repeated Across Backend, Frontend, AI Schema, And Recorder
tasks.firstPrinciplesForce: TestStep is the product's shared language; mismatch across generation, editing, recording, validation, and execution is a product bug.
implementationNotes: Start with a documented checklist near supportedStepTypes if a full shared package is too heavy.
Consider a shared package only when the step language changes again or when frontend/backend type drift causes friction.
If implemented, keep runtime validation available to the backend and schema generation available to AI output constraints.
Avoid introducing monorepo package complexity unless it reduces real synchronization work.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a clear source of truth or checklist for every place that must change when adding a step type.
Frontend and backend step type definitions remain consistent.
AI output schema remains consistent with backend validation.
Recorder conversion remains consistent with supported step types.
notes: This is intentionally last because it may be unnecessary until step types change again.
Implemented in the lightweight form its own implementation notes recommend: no shared package or monorepo tooling; the step registry from design-004 remains the single source of truth.

backend/src/test-steps/step-language-sync.spec.ts turns the hand-sync checklist into executable drift detection: AI schema enum/fields, system prompt coverage, frontend TestStep union (read from disk), and recorderActionTypes subset.
Revisit a real shared package only if the step language starts changing frequently enough that the drift tests become churn.
